items.title: Dynamic Composer Options Powered by Node Capabilities in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update enhances the Coding Bridge composer by rendering dropdown options for provider, model, reasoning effort, and permission mode directly from the node's capabilities instead of hard-coding them. This change allows for seamless updates to the UI without redeploying the web app whenever new models or effort tiers are introduced. The implementation includes a new capabilities map and ensures that the UI remains flexible and responsive to backend changes.

items.title: Increase WebSocket Max Size for Image Payloads in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update raises the WebSocket maximum size to 32 MiB in the relay's uvicorn server, addressing issues with image payloads exceeding the previous limit. The change ensures that images up to 12 MB can be processed without connection drops, enhancing the reliability of the CodingBridge composer. Both Docker and Kubernetes deployments are affected by this single modification.

items.title: Enhance CodingBridge with Image Attachments in Nexior 🎉
items.summary: This update introduces the ability to paste and attach images directly in CodingBridge sessions, aligning with VibeBridge's features. Users can now share visual prompts by attaching images up to 12 MB, with a maximum of 8 images per turn. The changes include updates to the composer, store actions, and transcript display, along with new internationalization keys for better localization support.

items.title: Dynamic Provider Management in Coding Bridge Agent 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new capabilities system that allows the node to dynamically advertise available providers, models, and effort tiers to the UI. By eliminating hard-coded values, the web app can now reflect changes instantly without requiring redeployments. The new structure enhances flexibility and ensures that the browser always displays the most current options available from the node.

items.title: Enhance Directory Selection with File Browser in Nexior 🎉
items.summary: This update introduces a new working-directory file browser, allowing users to easily select folders without typing absolute paths. The feature includes a `DirectoryDialog` that lists directories and files, supports parent navigation, and handles permission errors gracefully. Additionally, it enhances internationalization with new keys for 18 locales.

items.title: Streamline Download Page with Header-less Layout in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: The download page now features a new header-less layout, enhancing the visual appeal by removing the top navigation header. A dedicated hero section is now more prominent, while the shared layout remains unchanged for other pages. Additionally, the minimum height of the page has been adjusted for a better user experience.

items.title: Enhance Backend Switching and Reasoning Effort in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a backend selector allowing users to switch between Claude and Codex, along with a reasoning-effort selector for both backends. The session header now displays the active backend, and the model dropdown adjusts based on the selected backend. Additionally, 9 new internationalization keys have been added to support 18 locales.

items.title: Enhance I/O with Remote Directory Browser & Image Input in Coding Bridge Agent 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces two essential I/O features for the Coding Bridge Agent: a remote directory browser and image input support. The new `fs.list` action allows users to browse directories and select working paths, while the `session.start` and `session.send` methods now accept images in various formats. These enhancements improve user experience and functionality, ensuring better integration with existing providers.

items.title: New Coding Bridge Sidebar Entry Added in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new 'Coding Bridge' entry in the sidebar navigation, allowing users to access the `/coding-bridge` page directly from the UI. The feature is gated by a site feature flag and can be enabled or disabled by site admins through the settings. Additionally, internationalization support has been added for all 18 locales, ensuring consistent user experience across different languages.

items.title: Add codingBridge default feature in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update registers a new `codingBridge` entry in the site feature defaults, allowing the Nexior sidebar toggle to function with a clear platform default. The feature is disabled by default, enabling site admins to opt in via the Settings toggle. Legacy sites will automatically adopt this new feature without disrupting existing admin settings.

items.title: Fix shared application task loading issues in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update resolves the issue where selecting a shared application in task-based services resulted in a 404 error and prevented task loading. The fix ensures that grantees use their own credentials instead of attempting to create new ones, which were causing the failures. As a result, all 15 factory-based services can now successfully load tasks for shared applications.

items.title: Revamped Mobile Download Page for a Fresh AceData Look in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: The mobile download page has been completely redesigned to align with the AceData brand identity, featuring a clean layout with teal accents and the AceData logo. It now supports dark mode and includes a clearer structure with updated content in multiple languages. Additionally, new icons and improved accessibility features enhance the user experience.

items.title: Enhance Composer UX with IME-Safe Features in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces significant improvements to the Coding Bridge composer, including a larger IME-safe send box that accommodates multi-line prompts. The model dropdown has been revamped to allow easy selection and custom input, while a new permission-mode selector enhances session control. Additionally, internationalization has been updated with new keys across 18 locales, ensuring a broader reach.

items.title: Exciting New Codex Provider and Features in Coding Bridge Agent 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a live Codex provider alongside the existing Claude Code, enabling a seamless provider switch and enhanced reasoning effort control. Key changes include the addition of a new Codex provider that drives the `codex exec --json` CLI, improved session handling, and comprehensive testing to ensure functionality. The integration enhances the agent's capabilities while maintaining a focus on local execution and security.

items.title: Enhance Coding Bridge Assistant Text with Markdown in Nexior 🎉
items.summary: This update transforms the assistant text events in the Coding Bridge transcript to render as markdown instead of plain text. By utilizing the existing VueMarkdown component, the assistant's output now supports headings, lists, and syntax-highlighted code blocks, improving readability and user experience. Additionally, styling adjustments ensure the text inherits the transcript's color scheme while maintaining a clean layout.

items.title: Updated License to AGPL-3.0 for Better Compliance in Coding Bridge Agent 🚀
items.summary: This update changes the project's license from GPL-3.0-or-later to AGPL-3.0-or-later. Key files such as LICENSE, NOTICE, README.md, and pyproject.toml have been modified to reflect this new licensing, ensuring that anyone offering the service must also provide the source code. This change enhances compliance for network-facing functionalities.

items.title: Enhance Shared Badge Visibility in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: The grantee 'Shared' badge on the application balance card has been updated from a subtle gray text to a solid amber warning pill. This change improves visibility, ensuring users can easily identify when a key has been shared with them. The update is purely visual and does not affect any existing internationalization keys.

items.title: Enhance Transcript Tool Rendering in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update improves the readability of the Coding Bridge transcript tool by implementing terminal-style rendering for shell commands and limiting the output display for tool results. Key changes include a new layout for tool use events, a muted inline subtitle for descriptions, and the addition of per-tool icons. These enhancements streamline the user experience and make the transcript more visually appealing.

items.title: Fix Conversation History Actions for Better Functionality in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update resolves issues with the Conversation History panel on the coding bridge. The actions `getHistory` and `getHistoryDetail` were missing from the default export, preventing Vuex from registering them. With this fix, the panel will now display correctly, and the refresh functionality will work as intended.

items.title: Enhance transcript accuracy by filtering out noise in Coding Bridge Agent 🚀
items.summary: This update improves the history reader by stripping tool-injected context from real transcripts, significantly reducing junk titles from 104 to just 5. Key changes include unwrapping Codex context messages and scanning for real text blocks in Claude messages, ensuring sensitive information is no longer leaked. The implementation is backed by 5 new regression tests, confirming the effectiveness of these enhancements.

items.title: Enhance Shared App Visibility and Balance Display in Nexior 🎉
items.summary: This update introduces a 'Shared' badge for applications granted to users, enhancing clarity on ownership. Additionally, it fixes the display of the effective balance by ensuring that grantees see a valid number instead of a blank value. These changes improve user experience by providing better visibility into shared applications.

items.title: Enhance Grantee Balance Visibility in Backend 🎉
items.summary: This update fixes the issue where grantees received a null value for their remaining balance. Now, the effective remaining amount is calculated based on the grantee's credentials, ensuring they see their available quota accurately. New tests have been added to validate these changes, enhancing the overall reliability of the application.

items.title: Streamline Grok Models by Removing Dead Families in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update removes non-functional grok model families that were causing HTTP 500 errors due to lack of valid upstream accounts. Key changes include the deletion of dead models from configuration files and the maintenance of price-safety invariants for the remaining models. The update ensures that only active models are retained, improving overall system reliability.

items.title: Streamlined Grok Model Families in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update removes several non-functional Grok model families that were previously advertised and billed, addressing the issue of HTTP 500 errors. Key changes include the removal of dead entries from the OpenAPI enums, cost rules, and model registry, along with updates to documentation across multiple languages. The grok API and associated tests have been adjusted accordingly, ensuring a smoother experience for users.

items.title: Streamline OpenAI Responses Probe for GPT Models in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update refines the continuous E2E probe for the OpenAI Responses API by restricting it to only GPT text models. The previous implementation included various non-text and slow models, leading to instability. With this change, the probe now reliably samples from 27 stable GPT models, ensuring consistent performance without affecting production routing or billing.

items.title: Scope Conversations to End-User, Not App Owner in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update resolves an issue where conversation lists returned different results for the same end-user due to the credential-as-authorization model. By introducing a new variable to correctly scope conversation history to the actual end-user, the fix ensures that users see their own chat history rather than that of the app owner. The changes have been tested and confirmed to work as intended.

items.title: Enhance Local Coding History Replay in Coding Bridge Agent 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces the ability to read and replay local coding history from Claude Code and Codex, ensuring that refreshing the studio page retains the conversation context. A new reader module normalizes transcripts into consistent event shapes for seamless integration with the existing browser viewer. Additionally, the replay functionality allows users to resume Claude sessions, enhancing the overall user experience.

items.title: Enhance History Sync & Replay in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new history sync and replay feature for the Coding Bridge studio page, allowing users to access past sessions from Claude Code and Codex without losing context during refreshes. A history drawer lists previous sessions, and users can replay or resume sessions seamlessly. The implementation ensures that only minimal data is cached, enhancing performance and user experience.

items.title: Update pairing claim URL to current Studio route in Coding Bridge Agent 🚀
items.summary: This update fixes the pairing claim link that was previously pointing to an obsolete route. The default claim URL template has been updated in the configuration files to reflect the new Studio route, ensuring users can successfully access the pairing link. Changes were made in the config file, environment example, and README documentation.

items.title: Enhance CORS Support for Studio in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update adds studio and hub hosts to the CORS allowlist for the coding bridge, ensuring that requests from https://studio.acedata.cloud are no longer blocked. The deployment environment's ALLOWED_ORIGINS has been aligned with runtime defaults, and the README documentation has been updated accordingly. This change improves accessibility for users interacting with the API from the studio environment.

items.title: Streamline Navigation by Removing Profile Page in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update completely removes the unused /profile page and its associated route from the Nexior application. Key changes include the deletion of the profile navigation entry, the profile route registration, and the profile component files. As a result, any direct access to /profile will now redirect to the not-found route.

items.title: Enhance Account Management with Delete Option in Nexior 🎉
items.summary: This update introduces a new **Delete Account** option directly in the avatar dropdown for iOS users, improving discoverability. The delete flow has been modularized into a reusable component, ensuring a consistent experience across the app. This change addresses user feedback and complies with Apple’s guidelines for account management.

items.title: Automate PyPI Publishing for Coding Bridge Agent in Coding Bridge Agent 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new GitHub Actions workflow that automatically publishes the `coding-bridge-agent` package to PyPI on every push to the main branch. The versioning follows a date-based CalVer format, ensuring unique builds without collisions. This enhancement streamlines the release process and makes the package readily available for users.

items.title: Introducing Coding Bridge Remote Control in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update adds the Coding Bridge feature to Nexior, allowing users to pair a local Coding Bridge agent for remote coding sessions. Key components include a new data layer, a Vuex store for state management, and a user-friendly UI for managing sessions and permissions. The implementation supports full internationalization across 18 locales, enhancing accessibility for a global audience.

items.title: Disable Midjourney by Default in Backend 🚫
items.summary: This update disables the Midjourney feature for newly initialized sites by setting `DEFAULT_FEATURES_MIDJOURNEY.enabled` to `false`. Key changes include updating the public API to ensure Midjourney is no longer listed among available services and documents. Existing tenant sites remain unaffected, maintaining their current configurations.

items.title: Launch of Node Daemon for Remote Claude Code in Coding Bridge Agent 🚀
items.summary: The new node daemon for the Coding Bridge Agent allows users to run Claude Code locally while controlling it through the AceDataCloud web app or mobile devices. Key features include local execution, outbound-only connections, and a secure pairing process that ensures user permissions are respected. This implementation is designed to enhance user privacy and security by keeping all code execution and file access local to the user's machine.

items.title: Revamped coding-bridge Implementation in Index 🚀
items.summary: This update clarifies that coding-bridge and Nexior are newly developed under the MIT license, with only the Node daemon adapting GPL code from VibeBridge. Key changes include reframing the project as a reference architecture and ensuring that coding-bridge is not mischaracterized as a GPL derivative. The licensing and reuse tables have also been updated to reflect these changes.

items.title: Enhance Mongo Archive Deployment with Resilience in Index 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a continuous TKE Deployment for the Suno Mongo archive job, ensuring persistent runner progress is saved to COS. Key additions include management helpers for one-shot and deployment operations, utilizing internal Mongo and COS endpoints in Hong Kong. The deployment is currently active with a durable checkpoint system for improved reliability.

items.title: Introducing Auto Top-up Technical Plan in Index 🚀
items.summary: This update adds a comprehensive technical plan for the Auto Top-up feature, detailed down to the function level in the new `.plans/AUTO-RECHARGE.md` file. The system will automatically recharge user balances using authorized payment methods when they fall below a set threshold, enhancing user experience by eliminating manual payment processes. Key components include a new data model, backend endpoints, and risk control measures.

items.title: Introducing coding-bridge: A New WebSocket Relay in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces **coding-bridge**, a stateful WebSocket relay service that allows authenticated browsers to control Claude Code and Codex sessions on users' local machines. Key features include secure Ace JWT verification, a robust MongoDB persistence layer, and a device-flow pairing mechanism. This service enhances user experience by maintaining tool execution on remote nodes while ensuring ownership isolation and security.

items.title: Introducing Coding Bridge Design in Index 🚀
items.summary: This update adds a comprehensive design document for the Coding Bridge, a system enabling remote control of Claude Code/Codex from web and mobile devices. Key features include a secure architecture leveraging Ace JWT and MongoDB, a QR device-flow for pairing, and a phased implementation plan. The project builds on the VibeBridge framework, ensuring robust permission management and multi-session capabilities.

items.title: Enable Auto Recharge by Default in Frontend Web ⚡
items.summary: The Auto Recharge feature is now displayed by default in the console sidebar, allowing direct access without feature flag restrictions. Additionally, the Auto Recharge button is visible on application quick access cards, enhancing user experience. Menu comments in both English and Chinese have been updated to reflect these changes.

items.title: Enhance OpenAI Image Edit Size Inference in Platform Service 🎨
items.summary: This update improves the inference of image edit sizes for OpenAI by deriving dimensions from the first reference image. It ensures that the inferred sizes are valid multiples of 16, adhering to gpt-image-2 limits, and forwards these sizes in edit requests. Key files have been modified and new tests added to validate the functionality.
